what_it_does: A living noticeboard guide that inventories previously unsurfaced front-half interface designs and built artifacts from the composition project and related repositories, flagging them as candidates for recollection's common-knowledge index.
observations: Title frames the item as a set of FINDS: an unsurfaced front-half interface corpus plus a built sibling spatial shell.
Lists six numbered FINDS (F1-F6) with file paths, repo paths, and module names.
F1 identifies a mostly-designed-not-built front-half interface corpus under docs/v-symbol-build/.
F2 enumerates 14 named surface-mode primitives drawn from LIVE-RESOLUTION-INTEGRATION §19.2.
F3 states a spatial grammar is already designed and that a built instance exists in a sibling repo at /home/tim/repos/counterpart/design; the path was verified but the content was not.
F4 lists built interface-shadow modules in overlay/src, described as real but roughly 5% fidelity.
F5 notes that the front-half circuit maps onto wildcard's 'Circuit'.
F6 references a built Realtime wizard in a Supabase repo from Dec-2025 with preview_composition, user_choices, and conversational interrupts.
Mentions overlay/MAP/ as the full per-file inventory of overlay/.
The file has no code symbols; symbols[] is empty.
standouts: A whole front-half interface design corpus had not yet been surfaced.
A built sibling spatial shell reportedly exists in counterpart/design.
overlay/src contains real but low-fidelity interface-shadow modules.
A Dec-2025 Supabase Realtime wizard may be an earlier built interface-resolution loop.
conventions: Follows the ledger YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, links, and history.
Uses the noticeboard guide format to present findings rather than code.
Labels claims as FINDS and distinguishes provisional from confident without asserting correctness.
References external files by absolute local paths and repo paths.
Flags cross-repo claims for recollection verification.
concerns: Several claims are explicitly provisional or awaiting verification (F3 content unverified, F6 'RECOLLECTION VERIFY').
Heavy reliance on absolute paths that can drift or become stale.
Only one formal link (sourced_from) is recorded despite many referenced artifacts.
notes: This file is a recollection prompt: a catalog of hidden front-half interface designs and built fragments that should be indexed as common knowledge.
questions: What is the precise definition of 'front-half' in this context?
Which of the listed built artifacts are currently running or maintained?
How do the 14 surface-mode primitives relate to existing code or interaction modes?
Is the Dec-2025 Supabase wizard still active or archived?
What is the intended next step after these FINDS are surfaced?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to surface unsurfaced interface-relevant designs and built things for recollection indexing, and the content matches by listing them as FINDS with paths and brief descriptions.
apparent_themes: Low-fidelity built shadows versus designed specs
intention_signals: It tries to prevent a large body of front-half interface design and early built prototypes from being overlooked, and urges the system to index them as shared context before continuing composition.
novelty: It treats a collection of design documents and half-built UI modules as 'finds' to be rescued and indexed, rather than as authoritative specs or discardable experiments.
contribution: A curated pointer list to front-half interface concepts, design files, built components, and historical loops that a fused system should know exist, so future work can account for them.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide cataloging six FINDS of unsurfaced front-half interface designs, surface-mode primitives, a built sibling spatial shell, low-fidelity overlay modules, circuit convergence, and a prior Supabase Realtime wizard, intended for recollection indexing.
intention_for_embedding: To surface and preserve awareness of existing front-half interface design and built artifacts across repositories so they can be incorporated into common knowledge.
